title: ornithopus sativus subsp. roseus
intro: Ornithopus sativus subsp. roseus, also known as the pink subclover, is a subspecies of Ornithopus sativus, a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region. It is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The pink subclover is also valued for its health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the pink subclover is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and urinary tract problems.
